episodes.title: North District Water Treatment Plant Upgrades - Phase II

documents.content: Application for construction permits at Parcel ID: NW-8892. Scope includes replacement of three high-volume pumps and installation of new UV sterilization units. Estimated project cost: $4,250,000. Architectural summary indicates a 120 sq. ft. above-ground equipment enclosure to be constructed of galvanized steel.

To: Director of Public Works. From: Engineering Division. Re: Hydraulic modeling for Phase II upgrades. The new pump configuration will increase system pressure by 15 psi during peak hours. No changes to existing easements required. Budget allocation: $4,250,000.

Preliminary Environmental Assessment for NW-8892. Noise levels during construction projected to remain under 65 dBA. No wetlands or protected habitats identified within the 50-foot construction zone. Waste water discharge permit remains in compliance with EPA standards.

Permit request for stormwater detention basin expansion at Parcel ID: SW-4401. Proposed expansion adds 2.5 acres of retention capacity. Contractor: Mid-City Engineering. Total projected expenditure: $1,890,000. Includes installation of 4,000 linear feet of HDPE piping.

Submission from Resident Association regarding drainage flow. Concerns raised about temporary sediment runoff during excavation. Engineering staff response confirms silt fence installation protocols will be strictly enforced. No changes to the approved construction timeline.

Transfer of utility easement rights for Parcel ID: SW-4401. From: City of Oakhaven, Department of Utilities. To: Mid-City Engineering, LLC. Consideration: $0.00 (administrative easement). Parcel ID remains under municipal ownership. Easement width: 10 feet.

episodes.title: Electrical Grid Maintenance - Substation B Upgrade

documents.content: Application for transformer replacement at Substation B, Parcel ID: EL-3310. New transformers rated at 15 MVA to support growing commercial load. Project budget: $3,100,000. Architectural plans show noise-reducing acoustic barriers around the new equipment.

To: City Council. From: Electrical Operations. Re: Grid redundancy improvements. Substation B upgrade will reduce outage probability by 40% in the downtown district. Maintenance shutdown scheduled for 02:00-06:00 on April 15. No property acquisition required.

Electromagnetic field (EMF) analysis for Substation B. Projected fields at property line remain well below FCC safety limits. Soil contamination tests prior to excavation returned negative results. Construction materials are 100% recyclable.

episodes.title: Fiber Optic Expansion Proposal - Residential Zone 7

documents.content: Application for trenching and conduit installation for fiber optic network expansion. Target area: Residential Zone 7, Parcel IDs: FO-101 through FO-115. Contractor: NetLink Solutions. Total estimated cost: $2,450,000. Route includes 8 miles of underground conduit.

Supplemental application for sidewalk restoration. Restoration of 12,000 sq. ft. of asphalt and concrete sidewalks damaged during initial trenching. Budget allocation for restoration: $180,000. Completion deadline: June 1, 2024.

To: Public Works Director. From: Telecommunications Coordinator. Re: Coordination with utility locators. All existing underground utilities (gas, water, electric) have been marked with paint. No conflicts detected with the proposed fiber route.

Permit request for structural reinforcement and pump replacement at Wastewater Station C, Parcel ID: WW-5522. Project scope includes concrete repair and installation of variable frequency drives. Total cost: $980,000. Timeline: 18 months.

Odor control impact assessment for Wastewater Station C. New ventilation systems will reduce hydrogen sulfide emissions by 90%. Air quality monitoring stations installed at perimeter. No impact on adjacent residential properties expected.

To: Procurement Office. From: Engineering Division. Re: Vendor selection for WW-5522. Bid #4402 awarded to Industrial Fluid Systems. Contract value: $980,000. Payment terms: 10% mobilization, 80% progress, 10% retention upon completion.

Application for LED streetlight retrofit across 450 intersections. Parcel IDs: LG-001 to LG-450. Contractor: BrightPath Municipal Services. Project budget: $1,650,000. Includes removal of 450 high-pressure sodium fixtures and installation of smart-grid capable LED units.

Transfer of old fixture inventory. From: City of Oakhaven. To: BrightPath Municipal Services. Consideration: $12,500 (scrap value). Items: 450 high-pressure sodium lamps and 450 ballasts. Disposal to be handled in accordance with hazardous waste regulations.

To: Finance Department. From: Utilities Manager. Re: Energy savings projection. The LED retrofit is projected to reduce annual electricity consumption by 60%. Estimated annual savings: $220,000. Payback period: 7.5 years.
